results.title: AI Engineer at Equifax
highlights:
AI Engineer at Equifax
[...]
# AI Engineer
[...]
at Equifax • Full-time
[...]
Equifax is seeking a visionary AI engineer to lead our technology transformation initiative. In this role, you will lead a talented team in architecting and deploying cutting-edge, cloud-native solutions for a large enterprise. You will be at the forefront of modern development, employing vibe coding concepts with AI-powered coding assistants like GitHub Copilot, Gemini, and accelerate innovation and build highly scalable, reliable, and performant APIs, microservices, and PaaS/SaaS platforms, including the ability to design, develop, and deploy AI agents in the google cloud platform. This role requires a deep understanding of both front-end and back-end technologies, combined with mastery of cloud infrastructure, containerization, microservices architecture and the agentic AI framework. You're not just a coder; you're an architect, a mentor, and a key driver of the team's technical vision. If you are passionate about solving complex problems and mentoring a high-performing team, we want to hear from you.
[...]
As our AI Engineer, you will be the driving force behind our technical vision and execution. Your core responsibilities include:
[...]
Implement Sophisticated AI Agents: Design, build, and deploy complex AI agents using LangChain and LangGraph. You will own the core logic that automates intricate decision-making within the claims lifecycle.
[...]
Master Prompt & Context Engineering: Design, test, and refine complex prompts and contextual data frameworks to ensure our AI agents perform with maximum accuracy, efficiency, and reliability.
[...]
Lead AI Research & Innovation: Stay at the bleeding edge of AI. You’ll be responsible for identifying, prototyping, and integrating the latest foundational models, RAG techniques, and agentic frameworks to solve unique business challenges.
[...]
Build for Production Scale on GCP: Engineer and operate our AI systems in a scalable, reliable production environment on Google Cloud Platform. Your work will directly impact millions of users.
[...]
Champion MLOps for Agentic Systems: Establish and lead best practices for the reliability, versioning, monitoring, and observability of our AI agents, using tools like Langfuse to ensure production-grade performance.
[...]
Collaborate to Deliver Impact: Partner closely with product leaders, data scientists, and other engineers to translate business needs into technical reality, ensuring our AI solutions are both innovative and effective.
[...]
Champion modern software development practices by actively using AI code-assist tools (e.g., Gemini code assists, Github Copilot, Claude code) to accelerate development cycles, generate documentation, improve code quality, testing, and monitoring & observability practices
[...]
### What You'll Bring (Experience & Technical Stack)
[...]
Experience in a dedicated AI/ML role, with hands-on experience in model integration, MLOps, and applying AI to solve business problems.
[...]
Direct experience architecting and building solutions with LangChain, LangGraph, or similar agentic AI frameworks.
[...]
In-depth experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), specifically its AI/ML services (Vertex AI, etc.).
[...]
Hands-on experience with LLM observability tools like Langfuse for monitoring and debugging agentic workflows
[...]
### What could set you apart
[...]
Strong expertise in Generative AI (GenAI), including hands-on experience with models like Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, or Llama.
[...]
You are adept at leveraging modern development tools, including AI-powered code assistants (like GitHub Copilot), to accelerate the development lifecycle and rapidly ship high-quality features
[...]
Experience creating and deploying AI agents to production environments.
[...]
You are passionate about the potential of AI, but you are grounded in the practical realities of building and shipping reliable, production-ready software.

results.title: AI Consultant - Equifax | BeBee
highlights:
AI Consultant - Equifax | BeBee
[...]
AI Consultant - Equifax | BeBeeSkip to main content
[...]
BeBeeBeBee
[...]
Back
# AI Consultant
Technology
Equifax
London, United Kingdom2 days agoUntil 22/07/2026
Full timeHybrid
SaveApply now
Adapt my CV to this job
[...]
### Job description
[...]
Our AI Consultant roles are unique. The ideal candidate is a rare hybrid, a scientist with strong technical skills in AI and machine learning, the programming abilities to scrape, combine, and manage data from a variety of sources and a statistician who knows how to derive insights from the information within. They will combine the skills to create new prototypes with the creativity and thoroughness to ask and answer the deepest questions about the data, what secrets it holds, and to push the boundaries of what is possible with big data.
#### **What You’ll Do:**
[...]
* Conduct in-depth analysis of data available to Equifax and its partners.
* Collaborate with product managers to conduct market research and validate product needs.
* Develop and test AI models and algorithms, utilizing platforms like Vertex AI and BQML.
* Contribute to the creation of business cases for proposed AI solutions.
* Evaluate the feasibility and potential impact of AI projects.
* Provide technical guidance and support to junior analysts.
* Be proficient in Python, stay up-to-date on the latest advancements in AI and machine learning.
* Utilize combined knowledge of data structures, analytics, algorithms/models, and strong computer science fundamentals to independently prepare datasets, conduct analytics, and develop deployable solutions
[...]
* Evaluate the technical work of peer and junior data scientists, guiding them on deliverable quality and accuracy
 
#### **What Experience You Need**
[...]
* Bachelor's degree (2:1 or above) in a numerical subject (Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Engineering).
* Solid experience in data analysis, machine learning, and AI development in a UK Credit Risk environment
* Hands-on experience with cloud-based AI platforms and tools.
* Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and SQL .
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
* Good communication and presentation and visualisation skills.
* Strong experience in a related analytical role
* Proven track record of designing and developing predictive models in real-world applications
* Experience with model performance evaluation and predictive model optimisation for accuracy and efficiency
 
**What Could Set You Apart**
* Experience with specific AI techniques, such as neural networks or natural language processing.
* Knowledge of the financial services industry.
* Contributions to open-source AI projects.
* Experience with data visualization tools.
* Passion for data science, data mining, machine learning and experience with big data architectures and methods
* A Master's degree in a quantitative field (Statistics, Mathematics, Economics)
 
Keywords
[...]

results.title: Senior Data Engineer - RevOps Careers
highlights:
Senior Data Engineer - RevOps Careers

# Senior Data Engineer

Hybrid Full Time Alpharetta, Georgia, United States Equifax

You will play a crucial role in the evolution of our data platform, support the activation of AI capabilities across the team, and build data products and self-service solutions for the users of our data platform.

## Requirements

- Post a Job
- 5+ years of experience in data engineering, data science, data architecture, or a related data field
- Strong understanding of data engineering principles and best practices
- 5+ years experience working with software design with Java, Python, SQL, and JavaScript programming languages
- 5+ years experience with software build management tools like Maven or Gradle
- 5+ years experience with Cloud technology: GCP, AWS, or Azure
- Proficiency with continuous integration and continuous delivery tooling and practices

## Benefits

- Comprehensive compensation package
- Healthcare package
- 401k matching
- Paid time off

To apply for this job please visit equifax.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com.

#### Qualifications

- BS degree in a STEM major or equivalent discipline

What you’ll do

results.title: Equifax Data Analyst Jobs 2026 – Pune | Apply Now
highlights:
Equifax Hiring Data
[...]
Apply Now for
[...]
026 Openings
[...]
Equifax, one of the three largest credit bureaus globally, is expanding its Data & Analytics team in Pune, India. This Data Analyst role offers experienced professionals and strong freshers with 2+ years of hands-on exposure a chance to work on large-scale consumer data, build scalable analytics pipelines, and influence business decisions. With the rise of alternative credit data and AI-driven risk models, Equifax is investing heavily in analytical talent that can bridge data engineering and business insight. This role sits at the intersection of cloud analytics, data governance, and stakeholder communication—making it a strategic hire for 2026.
[...]
Candidates must hold a BS degree in a STEM major (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) or an equivalent discipline. While the official requirement mentions 2–5 years of experience, Equifax often considers exceptional freshers with strong project work or internships in data analysis, especially if they have cloud certifications. The role demands proficiency in SQL, BigQuery, R, Python, MS Excel/Google Sheets, Tableau, and Looker. A cloud certification (AWS, GCP, or Azure) is strongly preferred. Candidates with hands-on experience in cleaning, transforming, and analyzing large datasets from multiple sources will stand out. Equifax values practical coding and data storytelling over pure academic background.
[...]
- Independently produce descriptive and diagnostic analysis with minimal supervision
[...]
- Write efficient, scalable, and repeatable code against large datasets
[...]
- Build and optimize automated reporting pipelines to reduce manual intervention
[...]
- Integrate data from Equifax, customer, and third-party sources for complex analytical problem-solving
[...]
- Review code outputs for anomalies, perform root-cause analysis, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to fix issues
[...]
- Communicate business impact of findings to stakeholders and recommend data-driven actions
[...]
- Ensure compliance with data governance and usage policies
[...]
With the global push toward open banking and alternative credit scoring, Equifax is revamping its data infrastructure. This has created a surge in demand for analysts who can work across SQL, Python, and cloud platforms like BigQuery. The company is moving away from legacy on-premise tools to scalable cloud-based analytics. This role is not just about writing queries—it’s about building automated pipelines that reduce manual reporting. Analysts who can code efficiently and communicate insights to non-technical stakeholders will find this role a strong career accelerator.
[...]
The job posting explicitly states ‘Cloud certification strongly preferred.’ In practice, this means candidates with a GCP Associate Cloud Engineer or AWS Certified Data Analytics certification will jump ahead of equally skilled peers. Equifax uses BigQuery heavily, so familiarity with Google Cloud’s data services is a direct match. If you’re a fresher or have just 2 years of experience, investing 2–3 months to earn a cloud certification can significantly boost your chances. Companies like Equifax receive thousands of applications—certifications act as a trusted signal to recruiters.
[...]
a Data Analyst with 2–5 years of experience, Equifax typically offers ₹5–9 LPA in Pune, plus benefits like health insurance and stock options. However, this role often leads to senior analyst or data scientist positions within 1–2 years if you deliver strong pipeline automation. Equ
[...]
has a structured internal mobility program, and Pune is a key analytics hub for the company. Compared to similar roles at firms like TransUnion or Experian, Equifax’s compensation is competitive, but the real value lies in exposure to massive, real-world credit data and modern cloud tools.
[...]
Ensure your resume highlights SQL, Python, Tableau, and any cloud certification

results.title: Equifax Software Engineer - Entry Level | Pune - Meganaukri
highlights:
Equifax Software Engineer - Entry Level | Pune - Meganaukri

Job Role: Software Engineer – Entry

Company: Equifax

Job Location: Pune, Maharashtra, India

Job Type: Full Time

Job ID: J00176622

Work Mode: Hybrid

---

## Equifax Software Engineer – Entry Overview

Equifax is hiring Entry-level Software Engineers in Pune to work on meaningful projects using modern technologies. This role offers an excellent opportunity for early-career engineers to gain hands-on experience in cloud-native development, microservices, Java, and full-stack technologies while working in an agile environment at a global data and analytics leader.

This is a good entry-level software engineering role at Equifax for candidates with basic Java and Cloud exposure. Strong learning opportunity in a stable MNC with a modern tech stack. Best suited for candidates who have some hands-on Java + Cloud projects/internships. Apply quickly if you meet the minimum criteria.

---

## Roles and Responsibilities

- Design and develop small to medium-scale systems and modules
- Work with modern software development practices, including microservices and serverless architecture
- Participate in code reviews, design discussions, and agile ceremonies
- Integrate systems with existing corporate product platforms
- Contribute to technology roadmap and architecture discussions
- Apply CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, and automation practices

---

## Eligibility Criteria for Equifax Software Engineer – Entry

### Must-Have Requirements

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or equivalent
- 6+ months of experience with Java programming
- 6+ months of experience with Cloud technologies (GCP, AWS, or Azure)
- Strong fundamentals in software engineering principles

### Good to Have Skills

- Backend technologies: Spring Boot, Microservices, J2EE
- Frontend: HTML, JavaScript, Angular
- Databases: SQL Server, MySQL
- Tools: Git, Maven, Gradle, JIRA, Confluence
- Agile/Scrum experience
- Automated testing tools

---

## Equifax Selection Process

1. Online Application & Resume Shortlisting
2. Technical Assessment (Java + Cloud focus)
3. Technical Interview(s)
4. HR Discussion

---

## Get Job Alerts from Meganaukri

Receive verified IT job openings, fresher vacancies, internships,and off-campus hiring updates through our official channels.

Join WhatsApp Channel Join Telegram Channel

Only genuine and verified job updates are shared.

---

## How to Apply for Equifax Software Engineer – Entry

Official Apply Link: Apply Online – Equifax Careers

Documents Required: Updated resume highlighting Java and Cloud experience

---

## FAQs for Equifax Software Engineer Role

Who can apply?Candidates with a Bachelor’s degree and a minimum of 6 months of experience in Java + Cloud technologies.

Is this suitable for freshers?Yes. Candidates with relevant internships or academic projects in Java and Cloud are encouraged to apply.

Work Mode?Hybrid work setting in Pune.

Core Tech Stack?Java, Spring Boot, Cloud (AWS/GCP/Azure), Microservices, and modern development practices.

---

About EquifaxEquifax is a global data, analytics, and technology company that helps businesses and consumers make critical financial decisions with confidence. The company plays a vital role in the global economy through its data-driven solutions.

highlights:
# Equifax Hiring Java Developers 2026: Apply Now for Trivandrum Roles
[...]
Equifax, a global leader in data analytics and credit reporting, is expanding its technology hub in Trivandrum, India. As part of their 2026 recruitment drive, they are seeking experienced Java Developers to build and scale high-performance applications. This role is central to Equifax’s digital transformation, focusing on cloud-native development, microservices, and operational excellence. While the job posting targets candidates with 2+ years of experience, freshers with strong project portfolios and internship experience in Java, Python, and cloud platforms may also find this a compelling opportunity to join a data-driven Fortune 500 company.
[...]
| Company | Equifax |
| --- | --- |
| Role | Software Engineer – Java Developer |
| Location | Trivandrum, India |
| Experience | 2+ Years |
| Education | Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience |
| Salary | As per Company Standards (Estimated ₹6-12 LPA based on experience and market) |
[...]
Equifax requires a Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience. The role is specifically for candidates with 2+ years of hands-on experience in software design and programming with Java, Python, and JavaScript. Strong familiarity with build tools like Maven or Gradle is mandatory, along with solid frontend skills (HTML, CSS). The company values cloud experience (GCP, AWS, or Azure) and a proven ability to work in agile environments. For recent graduates, this means your academic projects, internships, or freelance work must clearly demonstrate these competencies. Equifax looks for self-starters who can independently manage priorities, so highlight any experience with UI frameworks (Angular, Bootstrap) and backend technologies like SpringBoot and microservices. The eligibility is strict on the 2-year experience benchmark, but exceptions exist for exceptional candidates with equivalent demonstrable skills.
[...]
- Develop and operate high-scale applications from backend to UI layer, ensuring operational excellence, security, and scalability.
[...]
- Apply modern software development practices including serverless computing, microservices architecture, CI/CD, and infrastructure-as-code.

Equifax’s selection process typically begins with an application screening via their careers portal or LinkedIn. Shortlisted candidates are contacted for an initial technical phone or video screen focusing on core Java, problem-solving, and system design. This is followed by a coding round (often on platforms like HackerRank) assessing algorithms, data structures, and practical coding skills. Successful candidates then face a panel technical interview covering Java internals, SpringBoot, microservices, cloud concepts (GCP/AWS/Azure), and frontend basics. A final HR round discusses cultural fit, compensation, and role expectations. The entire process can span 2-4 weeks. Common elimination points include weak fundamentals in Java concurrency, inability to design REST APIs, and lack of cloud experience. Equifax values candidates who can articulate their thought process and demonstrate a growth mindset.

Partner with account executives and the account team on proposals, negotiations, and contracting processes demonstrating extensive expertise on Equifax’s unique value proposition, value levers and ROI Understand the competitive landscape (competitors, pricing strategies, go to market approach), how Equifax solutions compare in the market and be able to articulate and defend our unique value proposition Present and validate use cases, demos and customer scenarios for which covered solutions are uniquely suited Gain deep and comprehensive perspective on industry challenges by networking with experienced industry peers and colleagues within own area of expertise Develop a strong perspective on the voice of the customer through consistent client interaction and feedback, acting as a conduit between customers and product teams, gathering feedback to inform product improvements, new features, and roadmap planning What experience you need Bachelor's degree in related field or equivalent work experience 5+ years of B2B sales experience, strong preference for demonstrated expertise in selling identity and fraud solutions Ability to travel and meet with clients in person (once a month on average) What could set you apart Excellent verbal and written communication skills and ability to convey in client meetings Expert problem solver, particularly in the development of new and intricate solutions Ability to manage multiple clients/project simultaneously Experience navigating a highly matrixed environment Understands and has experience with identity resolution in a digital and GLB use case Equifax is required by law to include a good-faith salary range for this role. This compensation range takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to: skill sets; experience and training; and other business and organizational needs. At Equifax, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role. A reasonable estimate of the current base salary range is $102,110.00 to $127,640.00. This position is also eligible for our sales incentive compensation program at approximately 55%. The application window is anticipated to close on April 24, 2026. This date is a good faith estimate only and may be modified where necessary. #LI-LMEFX #LI-REMOTE We offer comprehensive compensation and healthcare packages, 401k matching, paid time off, and organizational growth potential through our online learning platform with guided career tracks. Are you ready to power your possible? Apply today, and get started on a path toward an exciting new career at Equifax, where you can make a difference! Primary Location: USA-Idaho-Remote Function: Function - Sales Support Schedule: Full time

results.title: Data Science (Generative AI) - Capital One Auto Finance | Wfh
highlights:
# Data Science (Generative AI) - Capital One Auto Finance
[...]
Data is at the center of everything we do. As a startup, we disrupted the credit card industry by individually personalizing every credit card offer using statistical modeling and the relational database, cutting edge technology in 1988! Fast-forward a few years, and this little innovation and our passion for data has skyrocketed us to a Fortune 200 company and a leader in the world of data-driven decision-making. As a Data Scientist at Capital One, you’ll be part of a team that’s leading the next wave of disruption at a whole new scale, using the latest in computing and machine learning technologies and operating across billions of customer records to unlock the big opportunities that help everyday people save money, time and agony in their financial lives. As a Data Scientist at Capital One's Auto Finance business, you’ll be part of a high performing modeling and analytics team that’s leading the next wave of disruption at a whole new scale. Our team has a relentless focus on the craft of modeling and innovation, using the latest in computing and machine learning technologies and operating across billions of customer records to unlock the big opportunities that help everyday people save money, time and agony in their financial lives. You’ll drive the heart of business by working on an array of impactful and exciting applications like customer lifetime valuation, product recommendation, fraud detection, and productivity improvement via Generative AI. In this role, you will: Partner with a cross-functional team of data scientists, software engineers, machine learning engineers and product managers to deliver AI powered products that change how customers interact with their money. Leverage a broad stack of open source and cloud technologies to reveal the insights hidden within huge volumes of numeric and textual data. Drive the development of customer-facing Generative AI applications, including novel agentic AI solutions, by expertly finetuning Large Language Models and applying reinforcement learning. Build AI/ML solutions through all phases of development, from design through training, evaluation, and validation; partnering with engineering teams to operationalize them in scalable and resilient production systems that serve 80+ million customers. Flex your interpersonal skills to translate the complexity of your work into tangible business goals. The Ideal Candidate is: Customer first. You love the process of analyzing and creating, but also share our passion to do the right thing. You know at the end of the day it’s about making the right decision for our customers. Innovative. You continually research and evaluate emerging technologies. You stay current on published state-of-the-art methods, technologies, and applications and seek out opportunities to apply them. Creative. You thrive on bringing definition to big, undefined problems. You love asking questions and pushing hard to find answers. You’re not afraid to share a new idea. Technical. You’re comfortable with advanced ML and DL technologies including language models and are passionate about developing further. You have hands-on experience working with LLMs and solutions using open-source tools and cloud computing platforms. Influential. You are passionate about AI/ML and can bring along a cross functional team in breakthrough innovations. You communicate clearly and effectively to share your findings with non-technical audiences. You are experienced in training and fine tuning large language models or large multimodal models as well as have expertise in one or more key subdomains such as: Reinforcement Learning, Responsible AI, Computer Vision, RLHF. You have an engineering mindset as shown by a track record of delivering models at scale both in training data and inference volumes. You have experience in delivering libraries, platforms, or solution level code to existing products.

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

This role leads the strategic adoption of AI, particularly Generative AI, within USAA Federal Savings Bank to enhance productivity and reduce costs. The candidate will identify and evaluate external AI tools, develop intelligent work assistants, and deploy advanced analytics models. Requires 8+ years in predictive analytics, proficiency in Python or R, and experience with machine learning and statistical modeling.

Lead and execute advanced analytics projects utilizing machine learning, simulation, and optimization techniques to generate actionable insights.

Python R SQL NoSQL Machine Learning Statistical Modeling Regression Decision Trees Clustering Algorithms Support Vector Machines

About The CompanyUSAA is a leading financial services organization dedicated to serving the unique needs of military members and their families. Our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service, and trusted advice. We strive to be the preferred choice within the military community by upholding our core values of honesty, integrity, loyalty, and service. USAA is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive environment where our team members can grow and thrive. We also take pride in supporting active-duty military spouses, offering flexible work arrangements such as remote or hybrid options, in line with company policies and business needs. Our organization values innovation and continuous improvement, making us a dynamic and rewarding place to build your career.About The RoleWe are seeking a highly skilled Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to spearhead the strategic adoption and practical application of artificial intelligence, with a particular focus on Generative AI, within USAA Federal Savings Bank (USAA FSB). This pivotal role involves identifying innovative AI solutions, whether emerging industry technologies, existing tools from other USAA divisions, or integrated platform solutions, to enhance productivity, automate processes, reduce operational costs, and unlock new revenue streams in the financial services sector. The successful candidate will actively scout and evaluate external AI tools and technologies through various channels, including professional networks, academic collaborations, industry conferences, and research publications, ensuring USAA remains at the forefront of technological advancements. The role emphasizes the development of intelligent work assistants that streamline routine tasks, thereby improving the member experience and operational efficiency while considering ethical implications and future potential of AI innovations.This position offers remote work flexibility within the continental U.S., with occasional travel requirements. Candidates residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office are expected to work on-site four days a week. Relocation assistance is available for qualified applicants seeking to join our team.QualificationsThe ideal candidate will possess a strong educational background in a quantitative discipline such as mathematics, computer science, statistics, economics, finance, actuarial sciences, or engineering. A minimum of eight years of experience in predictive analytics or data analysis is required, with advanced degrees (Master’s or PhD) in relevant fields and at least six years of related experience preferred. Candidates should have extensive experience in training, validating, and deploying advanced analytics models, including machine learning, physical models, and statistical techniques.

results.title: Data Scientist - Specialty Equipment Services | Hirevector
highlights: in the insurance industry means opportunity for you. Great American Insurance Group's member companies are subsidiaries of American Financial Group. We combine a "small company" culture where your ideas will be heard with "big company" expertise to help you succeed. With over 30 specialty and property and casualty operations, there are always opportunities here to learn and grow. At Great American, we value and recognize the benefits derived when people with different backgrounds and experiences work together to achieve business results. Our goal is to create a workplace where all employees feel included, empowered, and enabled to perform at their best. The Specialty Equipment Services division is looking for a Data Scientist to build a modern data intelligence team that improves decisions, strengthens client value, and increases operational efficiency. The data scientist role blends strategy and hands-on delivery: you will partner with leaders to define the right questions, map current data realities, and deliver analytics and AI solutions that help us tell our story and drive results for both our clients and Great American Insurance. Specialty Equipment - Great American Insurance Group Great American’s culture is built on connection, shared learning, and strong relationships. To support this, employees in this role are expected to be on‑site four days a week, with the flexibility to work one day remotely. Core in‑office days are Tuesday–Thursday, with the fourth day determined by business needs. Essential Job Functions & Responsibilities Work closely with leadership team to identify key metrics and insights needed to support strategic goals and business objectives Develop and maintain financial models for large commercial clients and internal underwriting and leadership stakeholders Translate complex account structures, underwriting economics, and performance drivers into clear narratives and visuals; a storyteller mindset Perform gap analysis on current state, develop and recommend roadmap sequence of investments (projects, processes, technology enhancements) to achieve vision Drive innovation by exploring new tools, alternate data sources, modeling techniques to enhance business performance Lead development of an “insight” digital product for clients Guide training to promote data literacy skillsets for divisional employees Works closely with divisional leaders, shared services, and IT partners to ensure solutions are practical and adoptable Job Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field (or equivalent experience) Generally, 3 or more years of experience translating business needs into analytics deliverables, measurables, and compelling narratives Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to structure ambiguous problems and define success metrics Working knowledge of modern data practices (data sourcing, data quality, modeling, and governance concepts) Proficiency with analytical tools and languages appropriate for data science work (e.g., SQL and at least one modeling/scripting language) Ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders; strong presentation and writing skills. May require periodic travel to stakeholder sites or company locations (10% Travel) Preferred Qualifications Experience in insurance, financial services, or risk-oriented businesses Experience building client-facing analytics or ‘insight’ products Familiarity with cloud data platforms and BI tools used in the organization (e.g., Snowflake, Tableau, Power BI) Experience piloting and operationalizing AI use cases in partnership with IT and business teams Business Unit: Specialty Equipment Benefits: We offer competitive benefits packages for full-time and part-time employees*. Full-time employees have access to medical, dental, and vision coverage, wellness plans, parental leave, adoption assistance, and tuition

Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.The OpportunityWe are seeking a Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to drive the strategic adoption and practical application of AI, particularly Generative AI, across USAA Federal Savings Bank (USAA FSB). You will identify and implement AI solutions – whether cutting-edge industry advancements, existing solutions from other USAA lines of business, or tools that connect and integrate various platforms and IT systems – to boost productivity, automate processes, reduce costs, and create new revenue streams within the financial services context. This includes actively scouting for and evaluating external AI tools and technologies through various channels, such as professional networks and ties to academia, AI events, conferences, academic research, and a keen awareness of technological advancements, to ensure we are leveraging the most innovative solutions available. These efforts will encompass exploring AI solutions that serve as intelligent work assistants, streamlining and optimizing routine tasks for our employees, ultimately enhancing the experience and value delivered to our USAA members, while also considering the visionary potential and ethical implications of these advancements.This role is remote eligible in the continental U.S. with occasional business travel. However, individuals residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office will be expected to work on-site four days per week.Relocation assistance is available for this position.What You'll Do
[...]
- 6 years of experience in a predictive analytics or data analysis OR Advanced Degree (e.g., Master's, PhD) in mathematics, computer science, statistics, economics, finance, actuarial sciences, science and engineering, or other similar quantitative discipline and 4 years of experience in predictive analytics or data analysis.

### 📋 Description
[...]
• You will develop AI systems that automate dispute and chargeback handling using structured evidence and business logic, creating a better experience for our customers. • You will build models that automate refunds, getting money back to our customers faster. • You will build and maintain evidence extraction pipelines that process unstructured data using LLM-powered workflows to produce structured, actionable outputs. • You will prototype new modeling ideas, run offline experiments, and drive the best-performing approaches into production with appropriate risk controls. • You will collaborate across Engineering, Servicing Operations, Product, and ML Platform to define requirements, evaluate tradeoffs, and communicate results clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
[...]
### 🎯 Requirements
[...]
• You have a total of 2+ years of experience as a machine learning engineer • Strong Python skills and experience writing production-quality code • Experience building and evaluating models for tabular classification problems (preferably gradient-boosted decision trees like LightGBM/XGBoost/CatBoost). • Experience building applications with LLM APIs (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic), including structured extraction, prompt engineering, and orchestration frameworks like LangChain or LangGraph. • Familiarity with document and unstructured data processing (PDF/image extraction, text parsing, or similar). • Experience with ML lifecycle tooling for training orchestration, experimentation, and model monitoring (e.g., Kubeflow, Airflow, MLflow, or equivalent internal platforms). • Proficient in using AI-powered developer tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, or similar) to accelerate iteration, debugging, and code quality as part of day-to-day development workflows. • You have mastered taking a simple problem or business scenario into a solution that interacts with multiple software components, and executing on it by writing clear, easily understood, well tested and extensible code. • You are comfortable navigating a large code base, debugging others' code, and providing feedback to other engineers through code reviews. • Your experience demonstrates that you take ownership of your growth, proactively seeking feedback from your team, your manager, and your stakeholders. • You have strong verbal and written communication skills that support effective collaboration with our global engineering team. • This position requires either equivalent practical experience or a Bachelor’s degree in a related field

results.title: Standard Chartered Data AI Specialist 2026
highlights:
Standard Chartered Bank is launching its 2026 recruitment drive for the role of Specialist, Data, Analytics & AI – OTCR, based in Chennai. This role is part of the bank’s AI Centre of Excellence (CoE) and focuses on transforming risk management and regulatory compliance through advanced analytics, machine learning, and generative AI. As financial institutions increasingly adopt AI to automate compliance, detect financial crime, and enhance credit risk modeling, Standard Chartered is seeking fresh talent with hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects. The position offers a unique opportunity to work on high-impact AI use cases in a regulated banking environment, bridging the gap between data science and business outcomes.
[...]
Candidates must hold a degree in a quantitative field such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Engineering. A postgraduate degree or professional qualifications in analytics, risk, or compliance are advantageous. While the role is open to freshers, hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects (even academic POCs or personal projects) is a strong differentiator. The bank values background in solution architecture, data/ML engineering, or advanced analytics. For freshers, relevant internships or capstone projects in financial crime, credit risk, or compliance analytics will give an edge. The bank typically looks for candidates with strong analytical thinking, programming proficiency (Python, R, SQL), and familiarity with ML lifecycle management.
[...]
- Design and deploy advanced analytics, ML, and AI solutions for risk management and regulatory compliance.
- Identify and prioritize high-impact AI/ML use cases including financial crime, credit risk, operational risk, and compliance monitoring.
- Build robust, explainable models that comply with internal policies and external regulations.
- Embed models into business processes, ensuring trust, monitoring, and continuous improvement.
[...]
- Python
- R
- SQL
- Machine Learning (supervised & unsupervised)
- Natural Language Processing
- GenAI/LLM
- MLOps
- Data Governance
[...]
The selection process typically includes an initial resume screening, followed by an online assessment covering quantitative aptitude, logical reasoning, and basic data science concepts. Shortlisted candidates then face a technical interview focusing on Python, SQL, ML algorithms, and GenAI/LLM project experience. A final HR round assesses fit with the bank’s culture and risk awareness. The entire process may take 2-4 weeks. Given the specialized nature, candidates with demonstrable project work in NLP or LLMs have a higher chance of progressing.
[...]
The job description explicitly asks for hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects. In 2025-26, banks are racing to adopt generative AI for document review, automated reporting, and customer interaction. As a fresher, even a small project—like building a chatbot for compliance FAQs or fine-tuning an LLM for summarising legal documents—can significantly boost your application. The bank is looking for candidates who understand not just how to use pre-trained models, but also how to evaluate their outputs for bias, accuracy, and regulatory compliance. This is a niche skill that commands premium salaries and faster career growth.

# Data Scientist II - Fraud Reporting

Data scientist focused on fraud analytics and risk mitigation at a major financial institution, applying machine learning and econometric modeling to banking data.
[...]
Perform sophisticated analytics (statistical and predictive analytics, machine learning modeling, etc.) to provide actionable insights that improve business outcomes and minimize risk and also provide consultation to business leaders and other stakeholders on how to leverage analytics insights and build strategies around analytics.

Independently perform sophisticated data analytics (ranging from classical econometrics to machine learning, neural networks, and natural language processing) in a variety of environments using structured and unstructured data.

Independently write, document, and deploy custom code in a variety of environments (Python, SAS, R, etc.) to create predictive analytics applications.
